title: pellaea fendleri
intro: Pellaea fendleri, also known as Fendler's goldback fern or Fendler's cliff fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of antioxidants and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Pellaea fendleri include alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in salves and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
